events.title: IDF acknowledges GPS restrictions on northern border
events.one_line: Amid the Gaza war, the IDF said GPS signals would be restricted along Israel's northern border; regional spoofing rose sharply thereafter.

sources.title: NPR — Israel's GPS spoofing deters strikes, disrupts planes and apps
sources.note: Reports IDF 15 Oct 2023 acknowledgment of GPS restriction and the subsequent spoofing surge.

events.title: Business jet spoofed over eastern Mediterranean
events.one_line: A Bombardier Global 7500 flying Turkey-to-Cyprus reported GPS spoofing that placed its FMS position near Beirut, ~120 nm off track.

sources.title: Forbes (Eric Tegler) — GPS Spoofing in the Middle East Is Now Capturing Avionics
sources.note: Late-Nov 2023 eastern-Med spoofing case captured avionics; false Beirut position.

events.title: UK Defence Secretary's RAF jet jammed near Kaliningrad
events.one_line: An RAF jet carrying Grant Shapps briefly lost GPS while flying near Kaliningrad on a Poland-to-UK flight.

sources.title: C4ISRNET — UK defense minister's plane has GPS jammed near Russian territory
sources.note: Reports the ~30-min GPS jamming episode near Kaliningrad (flight on 13 Mar 2024).

sources.title: BBC News — Grant Shapps plane's GPS signal 'jammed' near Russia's Kaliningrad
sources.note: BBC corroboration of the Kaliningrad jamming incident.

events.title: Finnair suspends flights to Tartu over GPS interference
events.one_line: Finnair paused daily flights to Tartu, Estonia (29 Apr-31 May 2024) after GPS interference blocked its GPS-based approach; two flights had diverted.

sources.title: ERR — Finnair suspends flights to Tartu for 1 month to seek GPS jamming solution
sources.note: Estonian public broadcaster; suspension dates and GPS-approach dependency.

sources.title: International Airport Review — Finnair suspends flights to Tartu for a month following GPS interference
sources.note: Corroborates one-month suspension and diverted flights.

events.title: North Korea GPS jamming episode near the NLL
events.one_line: South Korea reported hundreds of aircraft and ships hit by suspected North Korean GPS jamming near the Northern Limit Line over late May-early June 2024.

sources.title: 38 North — North Korean Non-kinetic Attacks: A Problem for Incheon Airport
sources.note: Details the 29 May-2 Jun 2024 jamming episode; 201 aircraft / 731 ships figures.

events.title: Ukraine strikes Black Sea platform linked to GPS spoofing
events.one_line: Ukrainian forces said they struck a former offshore gas platform that Russian forces had used for GPS spoofing in the Black Sea.

sources.title: Baird Maritime — Ukraine claims offshore platform in Black Sea used by Russia for GPS spoofing
sources.note: Ukrainian claim of Aug 2024 strike on a platform used for spoofing.

events.title: South Korea blames North Korea for GPS jamming attack
events.one_line: South Korea's military accused North Korea of a GPS jamming attack affecting civilian aircraft and ships over consecutive days in November 2024.

sources.title: Al Jazeera — South Korea's military blames North Korea for GPS signal 'jamming attack'
sources.note: ROK JCS attribution; jamming over 8-9 Nov 2024 affecting civil aircraft/ships.

events.title: Estlink 2 / Baltic cable damage; Eagle S detained
events.one_line: The Estlink 2 power cable and several telecom cables were damaged; Finland detained the tanker Eagle S, suspected of dragging its anchor.

sources.title: NPR — Finland detains Russia-linked vessel over damaged undersea power cable in Baltic Sea
sources.note: 25 Dec 2024 cable damage; Eagle S detention; suspected anchor-drag sabotage.

sources.title: Wikipedia — 2024 Estlink 2 incident
sources.note: Timeline detail (Estlink 2 failure 12:26 EET, 25 Dec 2024) and cross-references.

events.title: MSC Antonia grounding near Jeddah (suspected GNSS spoofing)
events.one_line: The container ship MSC Antonia ran aground near Jeddah; reporting linked the grounding to suspected GPS spoofing/jamming (unconfirmed).

sources.title: The Maritime Executive — MSC Containership Aground in Red Sea is a Possible Victim of GPS Jamming
sources.note: 10 May 2025 grounding near Jeddah; framed as suspected/possible GPS-jamming victim.

events.title: Israel launches Operation Rising Lion against Iran
events.one_line: Israel began major strikes on Iranian nuclear and military sites on 13 Jun 2025, opening a 12-day conflict that coincided with a surge in Gulf GNSS interference.

sources.title: Britannica — 12-Day War (June 2025)
sources.note: 13 Jun 2025 start of Israeli strikes; 12-day conflict framing.

sources.title: House of Commons Library — Israel-Iran 2025: Iran's nuclear programme and military action
sources.note: Official UK research briefing on the June 2025 strikes and timeline.

events.title: EU sanctions Russia's 841st Electronic Warfare Center
events.one_line: The EU Council listed the 841st Separate Electronic Warfare Center over GNSS jamming and spoofing affecting the Baltic states.

sources.title: Consilium (EU Council) — EU lists nine individuals and six entities responsible for destabilising actions
sources.note: Official 15 Jul 2025 listing naming the 841st EW Center over Baltic GNSS jamming/spoofing.

sources.title: Militarnyi — Europe Announces Sanctions Over GPS Jamming in the Baltics
sources.note: Reachable summary of the 15 Jul 2025 sanctions and the named unit.

events.title: Von der Leyen's plane reports GPS jamming near Plovdiv
events.one_line: A jet carrying the European Commission president reported GPS loss approaching Plovdiv, Bulgaria; initial Russia-jamming claims were later disputed.
events.editorial_note: Retained as a disputed incident, shown with its rebuttal. Filed under black-sea, but the geographic fit is imperfect — Plovdiv is inland SE Europe, not the Black Sea littoral. If more Balkan / SE-Europe events accrue, the fix is a dedicated region rather than stretching this one.

sources.title: CNN — Plane carrying EU's top leader targeted by alleged Russian GPS jamming
sources.note: 1 Sep 2025 reported GPS jamming approaching Plovdiv; initial attribution to Russia.

sources.title: Euronews — Bulgaria U-turns on claim Moscow jammed GPS of von der Leyen's plane
sources.note: Later dispute/U-turn on the jamming claim; included to keep the record honest.

events.title: Hormuz interference surge amid Feb 2026 escalation
events.one_line: Renewed US/Israel-Iran military escalation coincided with a sharp surge in GNSS interference around the Strait of Hormuz affecting 1,000+ vessels.

sources.title: CNN — Ships and planes are vulnerable to GPS jamming; the Iran war reveals how bad
sources.note: Feb 2026 escalation and Hormuz interference surge; contested attribution.

sources.title: France 24 — Surge in GPS interference around Strait of Hormuz increases shipping risks
sources.note: Corroborates the early-2026 Hormuz interference surge.
